bucket)\n196\t\t\t\treturn\n197\t\t\t}\n198\t\t\t// Handle PutObjectRetention (PUT ?retention on object)\n199\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"retention\") && key != \"\" {\n200\t\t\t\th.PutObjectRetention(w, r, bucket, key)\n201\t\t\t\treturn\n202\t\t\t}\n203\t\t\t// Handle PutObjectLegalHold (PUT ?legal-hold on object)\n204\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"legal-hold\") && key != \"\" {\n205\t\t\t\th.PutObjectLegalHold(w, r, bucket, key)\n206\t\t\t\treturn\n207\t\t\t}\n208\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n209\t\t\t\t// Handle UploadPart (PUT ?partNumber&uploadId on object) — standard\n210\t\t\t\t// S3 clients PUT parts; routing this to PutObject silently stored\n211\t\t\t\t// each part as the whole object and left the B2 multipart upload\n212\t\t\t\t// empty, so CompleteMultipartUpload always failed with InvalidPart.\n213\t\t\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n214\t\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n215\t\t\t\t\treturn\n216\t\t\t\t}\n217\t\t\t\t// Check for CopyObject (has x-amz-copy-source header)\n218\t\t\t\tif r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\") != \"\" {\n219\t\t\t\t\th.CopyObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n220\t\t\t\t} else {\n221\t\t\t\t\th.PutObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n222\t\t\t\t}\n223\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n224\t\t\t\th.CreateBucket(w, r, bucket)\n225\t\t\t}\n226\t\tcase http.MethodHead:\n227\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n228\t\t\t\th.HeadObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n229\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n230\t\t\t\th.HeadBucket(w, r, bucket)\n231\t\t\t}\n232\t\tcase http.MethodDelete:\n233\t\t\t// Handle DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration (DELETE ?lifecycle on bucket)\n234\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"lifecycle\") && key == \"\" && bucket != \"\" {\n235\t\t\t\th.DeleteBucketLifecycleConfiguration(w, r, bucket)\n236\t\t\t\treturn\n237\t\t\t}\n238\t\t\t// Handle AbortMultipartUpload (DELETE ?uploadId on object)\n239\t\t\tif uploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\"); uploadID != \"\" && key != \"\" {\n240\t\t\t\th.AbortMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n241\t\t\t\treturn\n242\t\t\t}\n243\t\t\tif key != \"\" {\n244\t\t\t\th.DeleteObject(w, r, bucket, key)\n245\t\t\t} else if bucket != \"\" {\n246\t\t\t\th.DeleteBucket(w, r, bucket)\n247\t\t\t}\n248\t\tcase http.MethodPost:\n249\t\t\t// Handle multipart upload operations\n250\t\t\tuploadID := r.URL.Query().Get(\"uploadId\")\n251\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Has(\"uploads\") {\n252\t\t\t\th.CreateMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key)\n253\t\t\t} else if uploadID != \"\" {\n254\t\t\t\tif r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\") != \"\" {\n255\t\t\t\t\th.UploadPart(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n256\t\t\t\t} else {\n257\t\t\t\t\th.CompleteMultipartUpload(w, r, bucket, key, uploadID)\n258\t\t\t\t}\n259\t\t\t} else if r.URL.Query().Has(\"delete\") {\n260\t\t\t\t// DeleteObjects (bulk delete) - uses POST with ?delete query param\n261\t\t\t\th.DeleteObjects(w, r, bucket)\n262\t\t\t} else {\n263\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Unsupported POST operation\", 400)\n264\t\t\t}\n265\t\tdefault:\n266\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MethodNotAllowed\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Method %s not allowed\", r.Method), 405)\n267\t\t}\n268\t}\n269\t\n270\t// PutObject handles S3 PutObject with encryption.\n271\t// For small files (<10MB), it buffers in memory.\n272\t// For larger files, it uses streaming encryption via temp files to avoid\n273\t// loading the entire file into memory.\n274\tfunc (h *Handlers) PutObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n275\t\tctx := r.Context()\n276\t\n277\t\t// Check Content-Length header\n278\t\tcontentLength := r.ContentLength\n279\t\tstreamingThreshold := int64(10 * 1024 * 1024) // 10MB threshold\n280\t\n281\t\t// Use streaming for large files or unknown size\n282\t\tuseStreaming := contentLength < 0 || contentLength > streamingThreshold\n283\t\n284\t\tif useStreaming {\n285\t\t\th.putObjectStreaming(ctx, w, r, bucket, key)\n286\t\t\treturn\n287\t\t}\n288\t\n289\t\t// Small file: buffer in memory\n290\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n291\t\tif err != nil {\n292\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n293\t\t\treturn\n294\t\t}\n295\t\n296\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n297\t\n298\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n299\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n300\t\tif err != nil {\n301\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n302\t\t\treturn\n303\t\t}\n304\t\n305\t\t// Generate DEK and IV\n306\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n307\t\tif err != nil {\n308\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n309\t\t\treturn\n310\t\t}\n311\t\n312\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n313\t\tif err != nil {\n314\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n315\t\t\treturn\n316\t\t}\n317\t\n318\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n319\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n320\t\tif err != nil {\n321\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n322\t\t\treturn\n323\t\t}\n324\t\n325\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256\n326\t\tplaintextSHA := crypto.ComputePlaintextSHA256(plaintext)\n327\t\n328\t\t// Create envelope header\n329\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n330\t\tif err != nil {\n331\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n332\t\t\treturn\n333\t\t}\n334\t\n335\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n336\t\tif err != nil {\n337\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n338\t\t\treturn\n339\t\t}\n340\t\n341\t\t// Encrypt data\n342\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n343\t\tif err != nil {\n344\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n345\t\t\treturn\n346\t\t}\n347\t\n348\t\tencrypted, hmacTable, err := encryptor.Encrypt(plaintext)\n349\t\tif err != nil {\n350\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n351\t\t\treturn\n352\t\t}\n353\t\n354\t\t// Build envelope: header + encrypted blocks + HMAC table\n355\t\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + int64(len(encrypted)) + int64(len(hmacTable))\n356\t\tenvelope := make([]byte, 0, envelopeSize)\n357\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, headerBytes...)\n358\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, encrypted...)\n359\t\tenvelope = append(envelope, hmacTable...)\n360\t\n361\t\t// Compute plaintext ETag (MD5)\n362\t\tetag := backend.ComputeETag(plaintext)\n363\t\n364\t\t// Build metadata\n365\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n366\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n367\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n368\t\t}\n369\t\n370\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n371\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n372\t\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n373\t\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n374\t\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n375\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n376\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n377\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n378\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n379\t\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n380\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n381\t\n382\t\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied\n383\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n384\t\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, bytes.NewReader(envelope), int64(len(envelope)), meta); err != nil {\n385\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n386\t\t\treturn\n387\t\t}\n388\t\n389\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n390\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n391\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n392\t\t}\n393\t\n394\t\t// Record provenance\n395\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n396\t\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n397\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put\")\n398\t\t}\n399\t\n400\t\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n401\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n402\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n403\t\t}\n404\t\n405\t\t// Return ETag\n406\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n407\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n408\t}\n409\t\n410\t// putObjectStreaming handles large file uploads with streaming encryption.\n411\t// It uses a temp file to avoid loading the entire plaintext into memory.\n412\t// The process is:\n413\t// 1. Stream request body to temp file while computing SHA-256\n414\t// 2. Create envelope header with the computed SHA-256\n415\t// 3. Stream from temp file through encryption to B2 via io.Pipe\n416\t// 4. Clean up temp file\n417\tfunc (h *Handlers) putObjectStreaming(ctx context.Context, w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n418\t\t// Phase 1: Stream to temp file and compute SHA-256\n419\t\ttmpFile, err := os.CreateTemp(\"\", \"armor-upload-*.tmp\")\n420\t\tif err != nil {\n421\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n422\t\t\treturn\n423\t\t}\n424\t\ttmpPath := tmpFile.Name()\n425\t\tdefer os.Remove(tmpPath) // Clean up on exit\n426\t\n427\t\t// Compute SHA-256 while copying to temp file\n428\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n429\t\tteeReader := io.TeeReader(r.Body, plaintextHash)\n430\t\n431\t\tplaintextSize, err := io.Copy(tmpFile, teeReader)\n432\t\tif err != nil {\n433\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n434\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n435\t\t\treturn\n436\t\t}\n437\t\n438\t\t// Get the computed SHA-256\n439\t\tvar plaintextSHA [32]byte\n440\t\tcopy(plaintextSHA[:], plaintextHash.Sum(nil))\n441\t\n442\t\t// Seek back to beginning of temp file for reading\n443\t\tif _, err := tmpFile.Seek(0, 0); err != nil {\n444\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n445\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to seek temp file: %v\", err), 500)\n446\t\t\treturn\n447\t\t}\n448\t\n449\t\t// Phase 2: Get encryption keys\n450\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n451\t\tif err != nil {\n452\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n453\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n454\t\t\treturn\n455\t\t}\n456\t\n457\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n458\t\tif err != nil {\n459\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n460\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n461\t\t\treturn\n462\t\t}\n463\t\n464\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n465\t\tif err != nil {\n466\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n467\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n468\t\t\treturn\n469\t\t}\n470\t\n471\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n472\t\tif err != nil {\n473\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n474\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n475\t\t\treturn\n476\t\t}\n477\t\n478\t\t// Create envelope header\n479\t\theader, err := crypto.NewEnvelopeHeader(iv, plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize, plaintextSHA)\n480\t\tif err != nil {\n481\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n482\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create header: %v\", err), 500)\n483\t\t\treturn\n484\t\t}\n485\t\n486\t\theaderBytes, err := header.Encode()\n487\t\tif err != nil {\n488\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n489\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encode header: %v\", err), 500)\n490\t\t\treturn\n491\t\t}\n492\t\n493\t\t// Create encryptor\n494\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, iv, h.config.BlockSize)\n495\t\tif err != nil {\n496\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n497\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n498\t\t\treturn\n499\t\t}\n500\t\n501\t\t// Calculate envelope size\n502\t\tblockCount := crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, h.config.BlockSize)\n503\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n504\t\tenvelopeSize := int64(len(headerBytes)) + plaintextSize + hmacTableSize\n505\t\n506\t\t// Phase 3: Stream encrypt via io.Pipe\n507\t\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n508\t\n509\t\t// Start encryption goroutine\n510\t\tencErr := make(chan error, 1)\n511\t\tgo func() {\n512\t\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n513\t\t\tdefer close(encErr)\n514\t\n515\t\t\t// Write header\n516\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(headerBytes); err != nil {\n517\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write header: %w\", err)\n518\t\t\t\treturn\n519\t\t\t}\n520\t\n521\t\t\t// Stream encrypt the plaintext\n522\t\t\thmacTable, err := encryptor.EncryptStream(tmpFile, pw, plaintextSize)\n523\t\t\tif err != nil {\n524\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"encryption failed: %w\", err)\n525\t\t\t\treturn\n526\t\t\t}\n527\t\n528\t\t\t// Write HMAC table\n529\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(hmacTable); err != nil {\n530\t\t\t\tencErr <- fmt.Errorf(\"failed to write HMAC table: %w\", err)\n531\t\t\t\treturn\n532\t\t\t}\n533\t\n534\t\t\tencErr <- nil\n535\t\t}()\n536\t\n537\t\t// Build metadata\n538\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n539\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n540\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n541\t\t}\n542\t\n543\t\t// Compute ETag while we have the temp file\n544\t\t// We need to read the file again for MD5, but we already have SHA-256\n545\t\t// Use SHA-256 truncated to 16 bytes as ETag for streaming (non-standard but works)\n546\t\tetag := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:16])\n547\t\n548\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n549\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n550\t\t\tBlockSize:     h.config.BlockSize,\n551\t\t\tPlaintextSize: plaintextSize,\n552\t\t\tContentType:   contentType,\n553\t\t\tIV:            iv,\n554\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n555\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n556\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n557\t\t\tKeyID:         keyID,\n558\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n559\t\n560\t\t// Upload to B2 with prefix applied using streaming reader\n561\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n562\t\tif err := h.backend.Put(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, pr, envelopeSize, meta); err != nil {\n563\t\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n564\t\t\t// Check if there was an encryption error\n565\t\t\tselect {\n566\t\t\tcase encErrVal := <-encErr:\n567\t\t\t\tif encErrVal != nil {\n568\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n569\t\t\t\t\treturn\n570\t\t\t\t}\n571\t\t\tdefault:\n572\t\t\t}\n573\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload: %v\", err), 500)\n574\t\t\treturn\n575\t\t}\n576\t\n577\t\t// Close temp file\n578\t\ttmpFile.Close()\n579\t\n580\t\t// Check for encryption errors\n581\t\tif encErrVal := <-encErr; encErrVal != nil {\n582\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Encryption error: %v\", encErrVal), 500)\n583\t\t\treturn\n584\t\t}\n585\t\n586\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n587\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n588\t\t\th.manifest.RecordPut(bucket, key, plaintextSize, hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]), iv, wrappedDEK, h.config.BlockSize, contentType, etag)\n589\t\t}\n590\t\n591\t\t// Record provenance\n592\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(key) {\n593\t\t\tplaintextSHAHex := hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:])\n594\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, key, plaintextSHAHex, \"put-streaming\")\n595\t\t}\n596\t\n597\t\t// Invalidate list cache entries covering this key's directory\n598\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n599\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n600\t\t}\n601\t\n602\t\t// Return ETag\n603\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n604\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Streaming\", \"true\")\n605\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n606\t}\n607\t\n608\t// GetObject handles S3 GetObject with decryption and range support.\n609\tfunc (h *Handlers) GetObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n610\t\tctx := r.Context()\n611\t\n612\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n613\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n614\t\n615\t\t// Get metadata first\n616\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n617\t\tif err != nil {\n618\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n619\t\t\treturn\n620\t\t}\n621\t\n622\t\tif !info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n623\t\t\t// Check conditional request headers for non-ARMOR objects\n624\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, info.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n625\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n626\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n627\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n628\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n629\t\t\t\t} else {\n630\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n631\t\t\t\t}\n632\t\t\t\treturn\n633\t\t\t}\n634\t\n635\t\t\t// Passthrough for non-ARMOR objects\n636\t\t\tbody, _, err := h.backend.Get(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n637\t\t\tif err != nil {\n638\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object: %v\", err), 500)\n639\t\t\t\treturn\n640\t\t\t}\n641\t\t\tdefer body.Close()\n642\t\n643\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(info.Size, 10))\n644\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", info.ContentType)\n645\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, info.ETag))\n646\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n647\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n648\t\t\tio.Copy(w, body)\n649\t\t\treturn\n650\t\t}\n651\t\n652\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n653\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata)\n654\t\tif !ok {\n655\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n656\t\t\treturn\n657\t\t}\n658\t\n659\t\t// Get the MEK for this object using the key ID from metadata\n660\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n661\t\tif err != nil {\n662\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n663\t\t\treturn\n664\t\t}\n665\t\n666\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n667\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n668\t\tif err != nil {\n669\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n670\t\t\treturn\n671\t\t}\n672\t\n673\t\t// Create decryptor\n674\t\tdecryptor, err := crypto.NewDecryptor(dek, armorMeta.IV, armorMeta.BlockSize)\n675\t\tif err != nil {\n676\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create decryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n677\t\t\treturn\n678\t\t}\n679\t\n680\t\tplaintextSize := armorMeta.PlaintextSize\n681\t\n682\t\t// Check conditional request headers\n683\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, armorMeta.ETag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n684\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n685\t\t\t\t// 304 Not Modified - set headers but no body\n686\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n687\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n688\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n689\t\t\t} else {\n690\t\t\t\t// 412 Precondition Failed\n691\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n692\t\t\t}\n693\t\t\treturn\n694\t\t}\n695\t\n696\t\t// Check if this is a multipart object (HMAC table in sidecar, no embedded header)\n697\t\tisMultipart := info.Metadata[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] == \"true\"\n698\t\n699\t\t// Check for range request\n700\t\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n701\t\tif rangeHeader != \"\" {\n702\t\t\th.handleRangeRequest(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified, isMultipart)\n703\t\t\treturn\n704\t\t}\n705\t\n706\t\t// Full object download with pipelined stream decryption\n707\t\th.handleFullObjectStream(w, r, bucket, key, decryptor, armorMeta, plaintextSize, info.LastModified, isMultipart)\n708\t}\n709\t\n710\t// handleFullObjectStream handles full object downloads with pipelined stream decryption.\n711\t// This uses io.Pipe to decrypt blocks as they stream from Cloudflare, reducing\n712\t// time-to-first-byte and memory usage compared to buffering the entire envelope.\n713\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleFullObjectStream(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time, isMultipart bool) {\n714\t\tctx := r.Context()\n715\t\n716\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n717\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n718\t\n719\t\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n720\t\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\n721\t\n722\t\tvar hmacTable []byte\n723\t\tvar dataBody io.ReadCloser\n724\t\tvar streamSize int64\n725\t\tvar header *crypto.EnvelopeHeader\n726\t\n727\t\tif isMultipart {\n728\t\t\t// Multipart object: HMAC table is in sidecar, no embedded header\n729\t\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n730\t\t\tsidecar, err := manager.LoadHMACTable(ctx, key)\n731\t\t\tif err != nil {\n732\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load HMAC table from sidecar: %v\", err), 500)\n733\t\t\t\treturn\n734\t\t\t}\n735\t\n736\t\t\t// Concatenate all block HMACs from sidecar\n737\t\t\tfor _, hmac := range sidecar.BlockHMACs {\n738\t\t\t\thmacTable = append(hmacTable, hmac...)\n739\t\t\t}\n740\t\n741\t\t\t// Data starts at offset 0 (no header), read only the encrypted data\n742\t\t\tstreamSize = plaintextSize\n743\t\t\tdataBody, err = h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\n744\t\t\tif err != nil {\n745\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), 500)\n746\t\t\t\treturn\n747\t\t\t}\n748\t\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n749\t\t} else {\n750\t\t\t// Single-PUT object: embedded HMAC table at end of file\n751\t\t\thmacTableOffset := crypto.HeaderSize + plaintextSize\n752\t\t\thmacTableSize := int64(blockCount) * crypto.HMACSize\n753\t\t\tdataSize := plaintextSize\n754\t\n755\t\t\t// 1. Prefetch HMAC table (small range read)\n756\t\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, hmacTableOffset, hmacTableSize)\n757\t\t\tif err != nil {\n758\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to prefetch HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n759\t\t\t\treturn\n760\t\t\t}\n761\t\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n762\t\t\thmacBody.Close()\n763\t\t\tif err != nil {\n764\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n765\t\t\t\treturn\n766\t\t\t}\n767\t\n768\t\t\t// 2. Start streaming data from Cloudflare (header + encrypted blocks, stop before HMAC)\n769\t\t\tstreamSize = crypto.HeaderSize + dataSize\n770\t\t\tdataBody, err = h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, 0, streamSize)\n771\t\t\tif err != nil {\n772\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get object stream: %v\", err), 500)\n773\t\t\t\treturn\n774\t\t\t}\n775\t\t\tdefer dataBody.Close()\n776\t\n777\t\t\t// 3. Read and discard the 64-byte header (single-PUT only)\n778\t\t\theaderBuf := make([]byte, crypto.HeaderSize)\n779\t\t\tif _, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, headerBuf); err != nil {\n780\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read header: %v\", err), 500)\n781\t\t\t\treturn\n782\t\t\t}\n783\t\n784\t\t\t// Parse header to get plaintext SHA for verification\n785\t\t\theader, err = crypto.DecodeHeader(headerBuf)\n786\t\t\tif err != nil {\n787\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode header: %v\", err), 500)\n788\t\t\t\treturn\n789\t\t\t}\n790\t\t}\n791\t\n792\t\t// 4. Set response headers before streaming\n793\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(plaintextSize, 10))\n794\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n795\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n796\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n797\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n798\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Stream\", \"pipelined\")\n799\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n800\t\n801\t\t// 5. Stream decrypt using io.Pipe\n802\t\tpr, pw := io.Pipe()\n803\t\n804\t\t// Start decryption goroutine\n805\t\tgo func() {\n806\t\t\tdefer pw.Close()\n807\t\n808\t\t\tplaintextHash := sha256.New()\n809\t\t\tencryptedBuf := make([]byte, blockSize)\n810\t\n811\t\t\tfor blockIndex := 0; blockIndex < blockCount; blockIndex++ {\n812\t\t\t\t// Calculate actual block size (last block may be smaller)\n813\t\t\t\tremaining := plaintextSize - int64(blockIndex)*int64(blockSize)\n814\t\t\t\tactualBlockSize := int(min64(int64(blockSize), remaining))\n815\t\n816\t\t\t\t// Read encrypted block\n817\t\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:actualBlockSize]\n818\t\t\t\tn, err := io.ReadFull(dataBody, encryptedBuf)\n819\t\t\t\tif err != nil && err != io.EOF && err != io.ErrUnexpectedEOF {\n820\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"read error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n821\t\t\t\t\treturn\n822\t\t\t\t}\n823\t\t\t\tif n == 0 {\n824\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n825\t\t\t\t}\n826\t\t\t\tencryptedBuf = encryptedBuf[:n]\n827\t\n828\t\t\t\t// Verify HMAC\n829\t\t\t\thmacOffset := blockIndex * crypto.HMACSize\n830\t\t\t\tif hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize > len(hmacTable) {\n831\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"HMAC table too short at block %d\", blockIndex))\n832\t\t\t\t\treturn\n833\t\t\t\t}\n834\t\t\t\texpectedHMAC := hmacTable[hmacOffset : hmacOffset+crypto.HMACSize]\n835\t\n836\t\t\t\tmac := hmac.New(sha256.New, decryptor.HMACKey())\n837\t\t\t\tindexBytes := make([]byte, 4)\n838\t\t\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(indexBytes, uint32(blockIndex))\n839\t\t\t\tmac.Write(indexBytes)\n840\t\t\t\tmac.Write(encryptedBuf)\n841\t\t\t\tcomputed := mac.Sum(nil)\n842\t\n843\t\t\t\tif !hmac.Equal(computed, expectedHMAC) {\n844\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"block %d: HMAC verification failed\", blockIndex))\n845\t\t\t\t\treturn\n846\t\t\t\t}\n847\t\n848\t\t\t\t// Decrypt block (need to use CTR stream)\n849\t\t\t\tdecrypted := make([]byte, n)\n850\t\t\t\tctr := makeCounter(armorMeta.IV, uint32(blockIndex))\n851\t\t\t\tstream := cipher.NewCTR(decryptor.CipherBlock(), ctr)\n852\t\t\t\tstream.XORKeyStream(decrypted, encryptedBuf)\n853\t\n854\t\t\t\t// Update plaintext hash for verification\n855\t\t\t\tplaintextHash.Write(decrypted)\n856\t\n857\t\t\t\t// Write plaintext to pipe\n858\t\t\t\tif _, err := pw.Write(decrypted); err != nil {\n859\t\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"write error at block %d: %w\", blockIndex, err))\n860\t\t\t\t\treturn\n861\t\t\t\t}\n862\t\t\t}\n863\t\n864\t\t\t// Verify plaintext SHA-256\n865\t\t\tcomputedSHA := plaintextHash.Sum(nil)\n866\t\t\tvar expectedSHA []byte\n867\t\t\tif isMultipart {\n868\t\t\t\t// For multipart objects, use SHA from metadata (placeholder until tracked during upload)\n869\t\t\t\t// See bf-1v2ehf: multipart currently stores empty hash\n870\t\t\t\texpectedSHA, _ = hex.DecodeString(armorMeta.PlaintextSHA)\n871\t\t\t} else {\n872\t\t\t\t// For single-PUT objects, use SHA from header\n873\t\t\t\texpectedSHA = header.PlaintextSHA[:]\n874\t\t\t}\n875\t\t\tif len(expectedSHA) > 0 && !bytes.Equal(computedSHA, expectedSHA) {\n876\t\t\t\tpw.CloseWithError(fmt.Errorf(\"plaintext SHA-256 mismatch\"))\n877\t\t\t\treturn\n878\t\t\t}\n879\t\t}()\n880\t\n881\t\t// Stream plaintext to client\n882\t\tio.Copy(w, pr)\n883\t}\n884\t\n885\t// makeCounter creates a 16-byte counter value from the IV and block index.\n886\tfunc makeCounter(iv []byte, blockIndex uint32) []byte {\n887\t\tcounter := make([]byte, 16)\n888\t\tcopy(counter[0:12], iv[0:12])\n889\t\tbinary.BigEndian.PutUint32(counter[12:16], blockIndex)\n890\t\treturn counter\n891\t}\n892\t\n893\tfunc min64(a, b int64) int64 {\n894\t\tif a < b {\n895\t\t\treturn a\n896\t\t}\n897\t\treturn b\n898\t}\n899\t\n900\t// handleRangeRequest handles range read requests.\n901\tfunc (h *Handlers) handleRangeRequest(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string, decryptor *crypto.Decryptor, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintextSize int64, lastModified time.Time, isMultipart bool) {\n902\t\tctx := r.Context()\n903\t\n904\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n905\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n906\t\n907\t\t// Parse range header (bytes=start-end)\n908\t\trangeHeader := r.Header.Get(\"Range\")\n909\t\tstart, end, err := parseRangeHeader(rangeHeader, plaintextSize)\n910\t\tif err != nil {\n911\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRange\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Invalid range: %v\", err), 400)\n912\t\t\treturn\n913\t\t}\n914\t\n915\t\t// Check if this is a Parquet footer request and we have it cached\n916\t\t// DuckDB reads footer in two steps: last 8 bytes, then footer body\n917\t\t// Footer is at the end of the file: [footer_metadata][footer_length (4B)][PAR1 (4B)]\n918\t\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 {\n919\t\t\t// This range includes the end of the file - could be a footer read\n920\t\t\tif footer, ok := h.footerCache.Get(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag); ok {\n921\t\t\t\t// We have a cached footer, serve from cache\n922\t\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(footer))\n923\t\t\t\tif start >= footerStart {\n924\t\t\t\t\t// Request is entirely within the cached footer\n925\t\t\t\t\toffset := start - footerStart\n926\t\t\t\t\tfooterEnd := offset + (end - start + 1)\n927\t\t\t\t\tif footerEnd <= int64(len(footer)) {\n928\t\t\t\t\t\tplaintext := footer[offset:footerEnd]\n929\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n930\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n931\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n932\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n933\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n934\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"X-Armor-Footer-Cache\", \"HIT\")\n935\t\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n936\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n937\t\t\t\t\t\treturn\n938\t\t\t\t\t}\n939\t\t\t\t}\n940\t\t\t}\n941\t\t}\n942\t\n943\t\tvar encrypted, hmacTable []byte\n944\t\tvar startBlockIndex int\n945\t\n946\t\tif isMultipart {\n947\t\t\t// Multipart object: load HMAC table from sidecar, no embedded header\n948\t\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n949\t\t\tsidecar, err := manager.LoadHMACTable(ctx, key)\n950\t\t\tif err != nil {\n951\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to load HMAC table from sidecar: %v\", err), 500)\n952\t\t\t\treturn\n953\t\t\t}\n954\t\n955\t\t\t// Concatenate all block HMACs from sidecar\n956\t\t\tfor _, hmac := range sidecar.BlockHMACs {\n957\t\t\t\thmacTable = append(hmacTable, hmac...)\n958\t\t\t}\n959\t\n960\t\t\t// Calculate block range for this request\n961\t\t\tblockSize := armorMeta.BlockSize\n962\t\t\tstartBlockIndex = int(start / int64(blockSize))\n963\t\t\tendBlockIndex := int(end / int64(blockSize))\n964\t\n965\t\t\t// Clamp to valid range\n966\t\t\tblockCount := int(crypto.ComputeBlockCount(plaintextSize, blockSize))\n967\t\t\tif endBlockIndex >= blockCount {\n968\t\t\t\tendBlockIndex = blockCount - 1\n969\t\t\t}\n970\t\n971\t\t\t// Calculate encrypted data range (no header offset for multipart)\n972\t\t\tdataOffset := int64(startBlockIndex * blockSize)\n973\t\t\tlastBlockEnd := int64((endBlockIndex + 1) * blockSize)\n974\t\t\tif lastBlockEnd > plaintextSize {\n975\t\t\t\tlastBlockEnd = plaintextSize\n976\t\t\t}\n977\t\t\tdataLength := lastBlockEnd - dataOffset\n978\t\n979\t\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks\n980\t\t\tencryptedBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey, dataOffset, dataLength)\n981\t\t\tif err != nil {\n982\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %v\", err), 500)\n983\t\t\t\treturn\n984\t\t\t}\n985\t\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n986\t\n987\t\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n988\t\t\tif err != nil {\n989\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %v\", err), 500)\n990\t\t\t\treturn\n991\t\t\t}\n992\t\t} else {\n993\t\t\t// Single-PUT object: embedded HMAC table at end of file\n994\t\t\t// Translate range to encrypted blocks\n995\t\t\ttranslation, err := crypto.TranslateRange(start, end, plaintextSize, armorMeta.BlockSize, crypto.HeaderSize)\n996\t\t\tif err != nil {\n997\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to translate range: %v\", err), 500)\n998\t\t\t\treturn\n999\t\t\t}\n1000\t\n1001\t\t\t// Fetch encrypted blocks and HMAC table in parallel using errgroup.\n1002\t\t\t// This cuts range-read latency nearly in half for cache misses.\n1003\t\t\tg, gctx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n1004\t\n1005\t\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n1006\t\t\t\tencryptedBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.DataOffset, translation.DataLength)\n1007\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1008\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n1009\t\t\t\t}\n1010\t\t\t\tdefer encryptedBody.Close()\n1011\t\n1012\t\t\t\tencrypted, err = io.ReadAll(encryptedBody)\n1013\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1014\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read encrypted blocks: %w\", err)\n1015\t\t\t\t}\n1016\t\t\t\treturn nil\n1017\t\t\t})\n1018\t\n1019\t\t\tg.Go(func() error {\n1020\t\t\t\thmacBody, err := h.backend.GetRange(gctx, bucket, prefixedKey, translation.HMACOffset, translation.HMACLength)\n1021\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1022\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to fetch HMAC table: %w\", err)\n1023\t\t\t\t}\n1024\t\t\t\tdefer hmacBody.Close()\n1025\t\n1026\t\t\t\thmacTable, err = io.ReadAll(hmacBody)\n1027\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1028\t\t\t\t\treturn fmt.Errorf(\"failed to read HMAC table: %w\", err)\n1029\t\t\t\t}\n1030\t\t\t\treturn nil\n1031\t\t\t})\n1032\t\n1033\t\t\tif err := g.Wait(); err != nil {\n1034\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", err.Error(), 500)\n1035\t\t\t\treturn\n1036\t\t\t}\n1037\t\t}\n1038\t\n1039\t\t// Decrypt range (DecryptRange internally calculates block indices from plaintext offsets)\n1040\t\t// For multipart objects, hmacTableIsFull=true (all HMACs from sidecar).\n1041\t\t// For single-PUT objects, hmacTableIsFull=false (only range HMACs from embedded table).\n1042\t\tplaintext, err := decryptor.DecryptRange(encrypted, hmacTable, start, end, plaintextSize, isMultipart)\n1043\t\tif err != nil {\n1044\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decrypt range: %v\", err), 500)\n1045\t\t\treturn\n1046\t\t}\n1047\t\n1048\t\t// Cache Parquet footer if this looks like a footer read\n1049\t\t// Footer is detected by: 1) Reading near end of file, 2) Data ends with \"PAR1\" magic\n1050\t\tif h.footerCache != nil && end >= plaintextSize-8 && len(plaintext) >= 8 {\n1051\t\t\tif backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n1052\t\t\t\t// This is a Parquet file, try to cache the full footer\n1053\t\t\t\t// If we just read the last 8 bytes, cache it for footer length detection\n1054\t\t\t\t// If we read more, it might be the full footer\n1055\t\t\t\th.cacheParquetFooter(ctx, bucket, key, armorMeta, plaintext, plaintextSize)\n1056\t\t\t}\n1057\t\t}\n1058\t\n1059\t\t// Set response headers\n1060\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(int64(len(plaintext)), 10))\n1061\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", armorMeta.ContentType)\n1062\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag))\n1063\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1064\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", lastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1065\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Range\", fmt.Sprintf(\"bytes %d-%d/%d\", start, end, plaintextSize))\n1066\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusPartialContent)\n1067\t\tw.Write(plaintext)\n1068\t}\n1069\t\n1070\t// cacheParquetFooter caches Parquet footer data for faster subsequent reads.\n1071\tfunc (h *Handlers) cacheParquetFooter(ctx context.Context, bucket, key string, armorMeta *backend.ARMORMetadata, plaintext []byte, plaintextSize int64) {\n1072\t\t// If we got the last 8 bytes, we can determine footer length\n1073\t\tif len(plaintext) == 8 {\n1074\t\t\t// This is the footer length read - cache a small marker\n1075\t\t\t// The actual footer will be cached on the next read\n1076\t\t\treturn\n1077\t\t}\n1078\t\n1079\t\t// Check if we have the full footer by verifying PAR1 magic at end\n1080\t\tif len(plaintext) >= 8 && backend.IsParquetFile(plaintext[len(plaintext)-4:]) {\n1081\t\t\t// We have at least part of the footer\n1082\t\t\t// Parse footer length from the last 8 bytes if available\n1083\t\t\tfooterRange, err := backend.GetParquetFooterRange(plaintext[len(plaintext)-8:], plaintextSize)\n1084\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1085\t\t\t\treturn\n1086\t\t\t}\n1087\t\n1088\t\t\t// Check if we have the complete footer\n1089\t\t\tfooterStart := plaintextSize - int64(footerRange.Length) - 8\n1090\t\t\trequestStart := plaintextSize - int64(len(plaintext))\n1091\t\n1092\t\t\tif requestStart <= footerStart {\n1093\t\t\t\t// We have the complete footer, extract and cache it\n1094\t\t\t\tfooterOffset := footerStart - requestStart\n1095\t\t\t\tif footerOffset >= 0 && int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8 <= len(plaintext) {\n1096\t\t\t\t\tfooter := plaintext[footerOffset : int(footerOffset)+footerRange.Length+8]\n1097\t\t\t\t\th.footerCache.Set(bucket, key, armorMeta.ETag, footer)\n1098\t\t\t\t}\n1099\t\t\t}\n1100\t\t}\n1101\t}\n1102\t\n1103\t// checkConditionalRequest evaluates conditional headers and returns the appropriate\n1104\t// response status. Returns 0 if the request should proceed normally.\n1105\t// Supports: If-Match, If-None-Match, If-Modified-Since, If-Unmodified-Since\n1106\tfunc checkConditionalRequest(r *http.Request, etag string, lastModified time.Time) int {\n1107\t\tifMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-Match\")\n1108\t\tifNoneMatch := r.Header.Get(\"If-None-Match\")\n1109\t\tifModifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Modified-Since\")\n1110\t\tifUnmodifiedSince := r.Header.Get(\"If-Unmodified-Since\")\n1111\t\n1112\t\t// Normalize ETag (remove quotes if present for comparison)\n1113\t\tnormalizedETag := strings.Trim(etag, `\"`)\n1114\t\n1115\t\t// If-Match: Return 412 Precondition Failed if ETag doesn't match\n1116\t\tif ifMatch != \"\" {\n1117\t\t\t// If-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n1118\t\t\tif ifMatch == \"*\" {\n1119\t\t\t\t// Match any existing resource - proceed\n1120\t\t\t} else {\n1121\t\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n1122\t\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifMatch, \",\")\n1123\t\t\t\tmatched := false\n1124\t\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n1125\t\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n1126\t\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n1127\t\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n1128\t\t\t\t\t\tmatched = true\n1129\t\t\t\t\t\tbreak\n1130\t\t\t\t\t}\n1131\t\t\t\t}\n1132\t\t\t\tif !matched {\n1133\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n1134\t\t\t\t}\n1135\t\t\t}\n1136\t\t}\n1137\t\n1138\t\t// If-Unmodified-Since: Return 412 Precondition Failed if modified since date\n1139\t\tif ifUnmodifiedSince != \"\" {\n1140\t\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifUnmodifiedSince); err == nil {\n1141\t\t\t\tif lastModified.After(t) {\n1142\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusPreconditionFailed\n1143\t\t\t\t}\n1144\t\t\t}\n1145\t\t}\n1146\t\n1147\t\t// If-None-Match: Return 304 Not Modified if ETag matches\n1148\t\tif ifNoneMatch != \"\" {\n1149\t\t\t// If-None-Match can be \"*\" (match any) or a comma-separated list of ETags\n1150\t\t\tif ifNoneMatch == \"*\" {\n1151\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1152\t\t\t}\n1153\t\t\t// Parse comma-separated ETags\n1154\t\t\tetags := strings.Split(ifNoneMatch, \",\")\n1155\t\t\tfor _, e := range etags {\n1156\t\t\t\t// Trim space first, then quotes (order matters for \" value\" case)\n1157\t\t\t\te = strings.Trim(strings.TrimSpace(e), `\"`)\n1158\t\t\t\tif e == normalizedETag {\n1159\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1160\t\t\t\t}\n1161\t\t\t}\n1162\t\t}\n1163\t\n1164\t\t// If-Modified-Since: Return 304 Not Modified if not modified since date\n1165\t\t// Only applies if If-None-Match is not present (per RFC 7232)\n1166\t\tif ifModifiedSince != \"\" && ifNoneMatch == \"\" {\n1167\t\t\tif t, err := http.ParseTime(ifModifiedSince); err == nil {\n1168\t\t\t\t// Use >= comparison per RFC 7232\n1169\t\t\t\tif !lastModified.After(t) {\n1170\t\t\t\t\treturn http.StatusNotModified\n1171\t\t\t\t}\n1172\t\t\t}\n1173\t\t}\n1174\t\n1175\t\treturn 0\n1176\t}\n1177\t\n1178\t// parseRangeHeader parses a Range header like \"bytes=0-1023\".\n1179\tfunc parseRangeHeader(header string, totalSize int64) (start, end int64, err error) {\n1180\t\tif !strings.HasPrefix(header, \"bytes=\") {\n1181\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1182\t\t}\n1183\t\n1184\t\trangeSpec := strings.TrimPrefix(header, \"bytes=\")\n1185\t\n1186\t\tif strings.Contains(rangeSpec, \",\") {\n1187\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"multiple ranges not supported\")\n1188\t\t}\n1189\t\n1190\t\tparts := strings.Split(rangeSpec, \"-\")\n1191\t\tif len(parts) != 2 {\n1192\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"invalid range format\")\n1193\t\t}\n1194\t\n1195\t\tif parts[0] == \"\" {\n1196\t\t\t// Suffix range: -500 means last 500 bytes\n1197\t\t\tsuffix, err := strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n1198\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1199\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1200\t\t\t}\n1201\t\t\tstart = totalSize - suffix\n1202\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1203\t\t} else {\n1204\t\t\tstart, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[0], 10, 64)\n1205\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1206\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1207\t\t\t}\n1208\t\t\tif parts[1] == \"\" {\n1209\t\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1210\t\t\t} else {\n1211\t\t\t\tend, err = strconv.ParseInt(parts[1], 10, 64)\n1212\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1213\t\t\t\t\treturn 0, 0, err\n1214\t\t\t\t}\n1215\t\t\t}\n1216\t\t}\n1217\t\n1218\t\t// Clamp end per RFC 7233: a range ending beyond the file size is satisfiable\n1219\t\t// as long as start is within bounds. DuckDB httpfs requests aligned blocks\n1220\t\t// (e.g. bytes=0-131071) regardless of actual file size.\n1221\t\tif end >= totalSize {\n1222\t\t\tend = totalSize - 1\n1223\t\t}\n1224\t\n1225\t\tif start < 0 || start >= totalSize || end < start {\n1226\t\t\treturn 0, 0, fmt.Errorf(\"range out of bounds\")\n1227\t\t}\n1228\t\n1229\t\treturn start, end, nil\n1230\t}\n1231\t\n1232\t// HeadObject handles S3 HeadObject.\n1233\tfunc (h *Handlers) HeadObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1234\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1235\t\n1236\t\t// Fast path: serve from the in-memory manifest index when available,\n1237\t\t// avoiding a B2 HeadObject round-trip entirely.\n1238\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1239\t\t\tif entry, ok := h.manifest.Lookup(bucket, key); ok {\n1240\t\t\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, entry.ETag, entry.LastModified); status != 0 {\n1241\t\t\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n1242\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n1243\t\t\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1244\t\t\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n1245\t\t\t\t\t} else {\n1246\t\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n1247\t\t\t\t\t}\n1248\t\t\t\t\treturn\n1249\t\t\t\t}\n1250\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(entry.PlaintextSize, 10))\n1251\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", entry.ContentType)\n1252\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, entry.ETag))\n1253\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1254\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", entry.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1255\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1256\t\t\t\treturn\n1257\t\t\t}\n1258\t\t}\n1259\t\n1260\t\t// Manifest miss or disabled: fall back to a B2 HeadObject call.\n1261\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n1262\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey)\n1263\t\tif err != nil {\n1264\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", \"Object not found\", 404)\n1265\t\t\treturn\n1266\t\t}\n1267\t\n1268\t\t// Determine ETag and content info based on encryption status\n1269\t\tvar etag string\n1270\t\tvar contentLength int64\n1271\t\tvar contentType string\n1272\t\tif info.IsARMOREncrypted {\n1273\t\t\tif am, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(info.Metadata); ok {\n1274\t\t\t\tetag = am.ETag\n1275\t\t\t\tcontentLength = am.PlaintextSize\n1276\t\t\t\tcontentType = am.ContentType\n1277\t\t\t} else {\n1278\t\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n1279\t\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n1280\t\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n1281\t\t\t}\n1282\t\t} else {\n1283\t\t\tetag = info.ETag\n1284\t\t\tcontentLength = info.Size\n1285\t\t\tcontentType = info.ContentType\n1286\t\t}\n1287\t\n1288\t\t// Check conditional request headers\n1289\t\tif status := checkConditionalRequest(r, etag, info.LastModified); status != 0 {\n1290\t\t\tif status == http.StatusNotModified {\n1291\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1292\t\t\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1293\t\t\t\tw.WriteHeader(status)\n1294\t\t\t} else {\n1295\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"PreconditionFailed\", \"Precondition failed\", status)\n1296\t\t\t}\n1297\t\t\treturn\n1298\t\t}\n1299\t\n1300\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Length\", strconv.FormatInt(contentLength, 10))\n1301\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", contentType)\n1302\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, etag))\n1303\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Accept-Ranges\", \"bytes\")\n1304\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Last-Modified\", info.LastModified.UTC().Format(http.TimeFormat))\n1305\t\n1306\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1307\t}\n1308\t\n1309\t// DeleteObject handles S3 DeleteObject.\n1310\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1311\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1312\t\n1313\t\tprefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(key)\n1314\t\tif err := h.backend.Delete(ctx, bucket, prefixedKey); err != nil {\n1315\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to delete: %v\", err), 500)\n1316\t\t\treturn\n1317\t\t}\n1318\t\n1319\t\t// Remove from manifest\n1320\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1321\t\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n1322\t\t}\n1323\t\n1324\t\t// Invalidate metadata cache and list cache\n1325\t\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n1326\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1327\t\t\th.listCache.InvalidatePrefix(bucket, path.Dir(key)+\"/\")\n1328\t\t}\n1329\t\n1330\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusNoContent)\n1331\t}\n1332\t\n1333\t// CopyObject handles S3 CopyObject with DEK re-wrapping for ARMOR-encrypted objects.\n1334\t// This supports:\n1335\t// - Renaming files (same bucket, different key)\n1336\t// - Copying files (potentially different bucket)\n1337\t// - Key rotation (re-wraps DEK with current MEK)\n1338\tfunc (h *Handlers) CopyObject(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, dstBucket, dstKey string) {\n1339\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1340\t\n1341\t\t// Parse copy source header\n1342\t\tcopySource := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-copy-source\")\n1343\t\tif copySource == \"\" {\n1344\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing x-amz-copy-source header\", 400)\n1345\t\t\treturn\n1346\t\t}\n1347\t\n1348\t\t// Parse source bucket and key\n1349\t\t// Format: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n1350\t\tsrcBucket, srcKey := parseCopySource(copySource)\n1351\t\tif srcBucket == \"\" || srcKey == \"\" {\n1352\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidCopySource\", \"Invalid copy source format\", 400)\n1353\t\t\treturn\n1354\t\t}\n1355\t\n1356\t\t// Apply prefix for backend operations\n1357\t\tsrcPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(srcKey)\n1358\t\tdstPrefixedKey := h.applyPrefix(dstKey)\n1359\t\n1360\t\t// Get source object metadata\n1361\t\tsrcInfo, err := h.backend.Head(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey)\n1362\t\tif err != nil {\n1363\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchKey\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Source object not found: %v\", err), 404)\n1364\t\t\treturn\n1365\t\t}\n1366\t\n1367\t\t// Check metadata directive\n1368\t\tmetadataDirective := r.Header.Get(\"x-amz-metadata-directive\")\n1369\t\treplaceMetadata := metadataDirective == \"REPLACE\"\n1370\t\n1371\t\t// Build response XML structure\n1372\t\ttype CopyObjectResult struct {\n1373\t\t\tXMLName      xml.Name `xml:\"CopyObjectResult\"`\n1374\t\t\tLastModified string   `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n1375\t\t\tETag         string   `xml:\"ETag\"`\n1376\t\t}\n1377\t\n1378\t\t// Handle ARMOR-encrypted objects\n1379\t\tif srcInfo.IsARMOREncrypted {\n1380\t\t\t// Parse ARMOR metadata\n1381\t\t\tarmorMeta, ok := backend.ParseARMORMetadata(srcInfo.Metadata)\n1382\t\t\tif !ok {\n1383\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to parse ARMOR metadata\", 500)\n1384\t\t\t\treturn\n1385\t\t\t}\n1386\t\n1387\t\t\t// Get the source MEK using the key ID from metadata\n1388\t\t\tsrcMEK, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(armorMeta.KeyID)\n1389\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1390\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get source decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1391\t\t\t\treturn\n1392\t\t\t}\n1393\t\n1394\t\t\t// Unwrap DEK with source MEK\n1395\t\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(srcMEK, armorMeta.WrappedDEK)\n1396\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1397\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1398\t\t\t\treturn\n1399\t\t\t}\n1400\t\n1401\t\t\t// Get the destination MEK for the target key\n1402\t\t\tdstMEK, dstKeyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(dstKey)\n1403\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1404\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get destination encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1405\t\t\t\treturn\n1406\t\t\t}\n1407\t\n1408\t\t\t// Re-wrap DEK with destination MEK (handles key rotation and cross-prefix copy)\n1409\t\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(dstMEK, dek)\n1410\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1411\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to re-wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1412\t\t\t\treturn\n1413\t\t\t}\n1414\t\n1415\t\t\t// Build new metadata with re-wrapped DEK and destination key ID\n1416\t\t\tnewMeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n1417\t\t\t\tVersion: armorMeta.IV,\n1422\t\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    wrappedDEK,\n1423\t\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  armorMeta.PlaintextSHA,\n1424\t\t\t\tETag:          armorMeta.ETag,\n1425\t\t\t\tKeyID:         dstKeyID,\n1426\t\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n1427\t\n1428\t\t\t// Handle REPLACE directive - copy custom metadata headers from request\n1429\t\t\tif replaceMetadata {\n1430\t\t\t\t// Check for new content-type in request\n1431\t\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n1432\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-content-type\"] = ct\n1433\t\t\t\t}\n1434\t\t\t\t// Copy any additional custom headers from request\n1435\t\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header {\n1436\t\t\t\t\tif strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-\") && !strings.HasPrefix(k, \"X-Amz-Meta-Armor-\") {\n1437\t\t\t\t\t\tnewMeta[strings.ToLower(k)] = v[0]\n1438\t\t\t\t\t}\n1439\t\t\t\t}\n1440\t\t\t}\n1441\t\n1442\t\t\t// Perform server-side copy with updated metadata\n1443\t\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, srcBucket, srcPrefixedKey, dstBucket, dstPrefixedKey, newMeta, true); err != nil {\n1444\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Copy failed: %v\", err), 500)\n1445\t\t\t\treturn\n1446\t\t\t}\n1447\t\n1448\t\t\t// Record provenance for the copy\n1449\t\t\tif h.provenance != nil && h.provenance.ShouldRecord(dstKey) {\n1450\t\t\t\t_ = h.provenance.RecordUpload(ctx, dstKey, armorMeta.PlaintextSHA, \"copy\")\n1451\t\t\t}\n1452\t\n1453\t\t\t// Invalidate cache for destination\n1454\t\t\th.cache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n1455\t\t\th.footerCache.Delete(dstBucket, dstKey)\n1456\t\n1457\t\t\t// Return success response\n1458\t\t\tresult := CopyObjectResult{\n1459\t\t\t\tLastModified: time.Now().UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1460\t\t\t\tETag:         fmt.Sprintf(`\"%s\"`, armorMeta.ETag),\n1461\t\t\t}\n1462\t\n1463\t\t\toutput, err := {\n1479\t\t\tmeta = make(map[string]string)\n1480\t\t\tif ct := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\"); ct != \"\" {\n1481\t\t\t\tmeta[\"Content-Type\"] = ct\n1482\t\t\t}\n1483\t\t\tfor k, v := range r.Header encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1517\t\tw.Write(output)\n1518\t}\n1519\t\n1520\t// parseCopySource parses the x-amz-copy-source header value.\n1521\t// Supports formats: /bucket/key or bucket/key\n1522\tfunc parseCopySource(copySource string) (bucket, key string) {\n1523\t\t// Remove leading slash if present\n1524\t\tcopySource = strings.TrimPrefix(copySource, \"/\")\n1525\t\n1526\t\t// URL decode the key portion (keys may contain special characters)\n1527\t\tif idx := strings.Index(copySource, \"/\"); idx != -1 {\n1528\t\t\tbucket = copySource[:idx]\n1529\t\t\tkey = copySource[idx+1:]\n1530\t\t\t// URL decode the key\n1531\t\t\tif decoded, err := url.QueryUnescape(key); err == nil {\n1532\t\t\t\tkey = decoded\n1533\t\t\t}\n1534\t\t}\n1535\t\n1536\t\treturn bucket, key\n1537\t}\n1538\t\n1539\t// enrichPlaintextSizes replaces encrypted on-disk sizes with plaintext sizes for\n1540\t// ARMOR-encrypted objects. B2's ListObjectsV2 response includes the encrypted\n1541\t// object size; we resolve the plaintext size using the metadata cache (if warm)\n1542\t// or a parallel HeadObject call per object (on cache miss). Each errgroup goroutine\n1543\t// writes to a distinct slice index so no mutex is needed.\n1544\tfunc (h *Handlers) enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx context.Context, bucket string, result *backend.ListResult) {\n1545\t\teg, egCtx := errgroup.WithContext(ctx)\n1546\t\tfor i := range result.Objects {\n1547\t\t\ti := i\n1548\t\t\tkey := result.Objects[i].Key\n1549\t\t\tif am, ok := h.cache.Get(bucket, key); ok {\n1550\t\t\t\tif am != nil && am.PlaintextSize > 0 {\n1551\t\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = am.PlaintextSize\n1552\t\t\t\t}\n1553\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1554\t\t\t}\n1555\t\t\teg.Go(func() error {\n1556\t\t\t\tinfo, err := h.backend.Head(egCtx, bucket, key)\n1557\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1558\t\t\t\t\treturn nil // non-fatal: keep encrypted size rather than failing the listing\n1559\t\t\t\t}\n1560\t\t\t\tif info.Size > 0 {\n1561\t\t\t\t\tresult.Objects[i].Size = info.Size\n1562\t\t\t\t}\n1563\t\t\t\treturn nil\n1564\t\t\t})\n1565\t\t}\n1566\t\teg.Wait() //nolint:errcheck\n1567\t}\n1568\t\n1569\t// ListObjectsV2 handles S3 ListObjectsV2.\n1570\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListObjectsV2(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1571\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1572\t\n1573\t\tprefix := r.URL.Query().Get(\"prefix\")\n1574\t\tdelimiter := r.URL.Query().Get(\"delimiter\")\n1575\t\tcontToken := r.URL.Query().Get(\"continuation-token\")\n1576\t\tmaxKeys := 1000\n1577\t\tif mk := r.URL.Query().Get(\"max-keys\"); mk != \"\" {\n1578\t\t\tif v, err := strconv.Atoi(mk); err == nil && v > 0 {\n1579\t\t\t\tmaxKeys = v\n1580\t\t\t}\n1581\t\t}\n1582\t\n1583\t\t// Apply the configured prefix to the prefix parameter for backend operations.\n1584\t\t// When ARMOR_PREFIX is set, the backend stores all keys with the prefix prepended,\n1585\t\t// but clients don't know about it. We need to prepend the prefix to the client's\n1586\t\t// requested prefix so the backend finds the right objects.\n1587\t\tbackendPrefix := h.applyPrefix(prefix)\n1588\t\n1589\t\tvar result *backend.ListResult\n1590\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1591\t\t\tif cached, ok := h.listCache.Get(bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken); ok {\n1592\t\t\t\tresult = cached\n1593\t\t\t}\n1594\t\t}\n1595\t\tif result == nil {\n1596\t\t\tvar err error\n1597\t\t\tresult, err = h.backend.List(ctx, bucket, backendPrefix, delimiter, contToken, maxKeys)\n1598\t\t\tif err != nil {\n1599\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list: %v\", err), 500)\n1600\t\t\t\treturn\n1601\t\t\t}\n1602\t\t\t// Resolve plaintext sizes for ARMOR-encrypted objects before caching.\n1603\t\t\t// B2 ListObjectsV2 returns encrypted (on-disk) sizes; clients like DuckDB\n1604\t\t\t// use these sizes to compute byte-range offsets, so they must reflect the\n1605\t\t\t// plaintext length.\n1606\t\t\th.enrichPlaintextSizes(ctx, bucket, result)\n1607\t\t\tif h.listCache != nil {\n1608\t\t\t\th.listCache.Set(bucket, prefix, delimiter, maxKeys, contToken, result)\n1609\t\t\t}\n1610\t\t}\n1611\t\n1612\t\t// Build XML response\n1613\t\ttype Contents struct {\n1614\t\t\tKey          string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1615\t\t\tLastModified string `xml:\"LastModified\"`\n1616\t\t\tETag         string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n1617\t\t\tSize         int64  `xml:\"Size\"`\n1618\t\t\tStorageClass string `xml:\"StorageClass\"`\n1619\t\t}\n1620\t\n1621\t\t// S3 spec: each CommonPrefixes entry is a separate wrapper element with one\n1622\t\t// Prefix child. Using []string with \"Outer>Inner\" produces an empty\n1623\t\t// <CommonPrefixes/> when the slice is nil, which the AWS SDK v1 parses as a\n1624\t\t// slice with one nil element — causing nil-pointer crashes in clients like\n1625\t\t// litestream. strippedPrefix})\n1671\t\t}\n1672\t\n1673\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(resp)\n1674\t\tif err != nil {\n1675\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", contains XML with a list of objects to delete.\n1783\tfunc (h *Handlers) DeleteObjects(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket string) {\n1784\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1785\t\n1786\t\t// Parse the DeleteObjects request XML\n1787\t\ttype Object struct {\n1788\t\t\tKey string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1789\t\t}\n1790\t\n1791\t\ttype DeleteRequest struct {\n1792\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n1800\t\tif err != nil {\n1801\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n1802\t\t\treturn\n1803\t\t}\n1804\t\n1805\t\tif err := len(deleteReq.Objects) == 0 {\n1811\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", \"No objects specified for deletion\", 400)\n1812\t\t\treturn\n1813\t\t}\n1814\t\n1815\t\t// Extract keys\n1816\t\tkeys := make([]string, len(deleteReq.Objects))\n1817\t\tfor i, obj := range deleteReq.Objects {\n1818\t\t\tkeys[i] = obj.Key\n1819\t\t}\n1820\t\n1821\t\t// Perform bulk delete\n1822\t\tif err := h.backend.DeleteObjects(ctx, bucket, keys); err != nil {\n1823\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"DeleteObjects failed: %v\", err), 500)\n1824\t\t\treturn\n1825\t\t}\n1826\t\n1827\t\t// Remove from manifest\n1828\t\tif h.manifest != nil {\n1829\t\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n1830\t\t\t\th.manifest.RecordDelete(bucket, key)\n1831\t\t\t}\n1832\t\t}\n1833\t\n1834\t\t// Invalidate cache for deleted objects\n1835\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n1836\t\t\th.cache.Delete(bucket, key)\n1837\t\t\th.footerCache.Delete(bucket, key)\n1838\t\t}\n1839\t\n1840\t\t// Build response XML\n1841\t\ttype DeletedObject struct {\n1842\t\t\tKey string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1843\t\t}\n1844\t\n1845\t\ttype DeleteResult struct {\n1846\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name        `xml:\"DeleteResult\"`\n1847\t\t\tXmlns   string          `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1848\t\t\tDeleted []DeletedObject `xml:\"Deleted\"`\n1849\t\t\tError   []struct {\n1850\t\t\t\tKey     string `xml:\"Key\"`\n1851\t\t\t\tCode    string `xml:\"Code\"`\n1852\t\t\t\tMessage string `xml:\"Message\"`\n1853\t\t\t} `xml:\"Error\"`\n1854\t\t}\n1855\t\n1856\t\tresult := DeleteResult{\n1857\t\t\tXmlns: \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1858\t\t}\n1859\t\n1860\t\t// If not quiet mode, include all deleted keys\n1861\t\tif !deleteReq.Quiet {\n1862\t\t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n1863\t\t\t\tresult.Deleted = append(result.Deleted, DeletedObject{Key: key})\n1864\t\t\t}\n1865\t\t}\n1866\t\n1867\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1868\t\tif err != nil {\n1869\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1870\t\t\treturn\n1871\t\t}\n1872\t\n1873\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1874\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1875\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1876\t\tw.Write(output)\n1877\t}\n1878\t\n1879\t// ListBuckets handles S3 ListBuckets.\n1880\tfunc (h *Handlers) ListBuckets(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\n1881\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1882\t\n1883\t\tbuckets, err := h.backend.ListBuckets(ctx)\n1884\t\tif err != nil {\n1885\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to list buckets: %v\", err), 500)\n1886\t\t\treturn\n1887\t\t}\n1888\t\n1889\t\t// Build XML response\n1890\t\ttype Bucket struct {\n1891\t\t\tName         string `xml:\"Name\"`\n1892\t\t\tCreationDate string `xml:\"CreationDate\"`\n1893\t\t}\n1894\t\n1895\t\ttype ListAllMyBucketsResult struct {\n1896\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"ListAllMyBucketsResult\"`\n1897\t\t\tXmlns   string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n1898\t\t\tOwner   struct {\n1899\t\t\t\tID          string `xml:\"ID\"`\n1900\t\t\t\tDisplayName string `xml:\"DisplayName\"`\n1901\t\t\t} `xml:\"Owner\"`\n1902\t\t\tBuckets struct {\n1903\t\t\t\tBucket []Bucket `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n1904\t\t\t} `xml:\"Buckets\"`\n1905\t\t}\n1906\t\n1907\t\tresult := ListAllMyBucketsResult{\n1908\t\t\tXmlns: \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n1909\t\t}\n1910\t\tresult.Owner.ID = \"armor\"\n1911\t\tresult.Owner.DisplayName = \"ARMOR\"\n1912\t\n1913\t\tfor _, b := range buckets {\n1914\t\t\tresult.Buckets.Bucket = append(result.Buckets.Bucket, Bucket{\n1915\t\t\t\tName:         b.Name,\n1916\t\t\t\tCreationDate: b.CreationDate.UTC().Format(\"2006-01-02T15:04:05.000Z\"),\n1917\t\t\t})\n1918\t\t}\n1919\t\n1920\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n1921\t\tif err != nil {\n1922\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n1923\t\t\treturn\n1924\t\t}\n1925\t\n1926\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n1927\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n1928\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n1929\t\tw.Write(output)\n1930\t}\n1931\t\n1932\t// CreateMultipartUpload handles S3 CreateMultipartUpload with ARMOR encryption.\n1933\t// It generates a DEK and IV, wraps the DEK, and stores the state in B2.\n1934\tfunc (h *Handlers) CreateMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key string) {\n1935\t\tctx := r.Context()\n1936\t\n1937\t\t// Get the appropriate MEK for this object key\n1938\t\tmek, keyID, err := h.keyManager.GetMEK(key)\n1939\t\tif err != nil {\n1940\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get encryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n1941\t\t\treturn\n1942\t\t}\n1943\t\n1944\t\t// Generate DEK and IV for this upload\n1945\t\tdek, err := crypto.GenerateDEK()\n1946\t\tif err != nil {\n1947\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1948\t\t\treturn\n1949\t\t}\n1950\t\n1951\t\tiv, err := crypto.GenerateIV()\n1952\t\tif err != nil {\n1953\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to generate IV: %v\", err), 500)\n1954\t\t\treturn\n1955\t\t}\n1956\t\n1957\t\t// Wrap DEK with MEK\n1958\t\twrappedDEK, err := crypto.WrapDEK(mek, dek)\n1959\t\tif err != nil {\n1960\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to wrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n1961\t\t\treturn\n1962\t\t}\n1963\t\n1964\t\t// Get content type\n1965\t\tcontentType := r.Header.Get(\"Content-Type\")\n1966\t\tif contentType == \"\" {\n1967\t\t\tcontentType = \"application/octet-stream\"\n1968\t\t}\n1969\t\n1970\t\t// Initiate multipart upload with B2 (no ARMOR metadata yet - that comes on completion)\n1971\t\tuploadID, err := h.backend.CreateMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, nil)\n1972\t\tif err != nil {\n1973\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n1974\t\t\treturn\n1975\t\t}\n1976\t\n1977\t\t// Save multipart state to B2\n1978\t\tstate := &backend.MultipartState{\n1979\t\t\tUploadID:       uploadID,\n1980\t\t\tBucket:         bucket,\n1981\t\t\tKey:            key,\n1982\t\t\tIV:             iv,\n1983\t\t\tWrappedDEK:     wrappedDEK,\n1984\t\t\tBlockSize:      h.config.BlockSize,\n1985\t\t\tCreated:        time.Now(),\n1986\t\t\tContentType:    contentType,\n1987\t\t\tKeyID:          keyID,\n1988\t\t\tEncryptedBytes: 0,\n1989\t\t\tPartHMACs:      make(map[int]string),\n1990\t\t\tPartSizes:      make(map[int]int64),\n1991\t\t}\n1992\t\n1993\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n1994\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n1995\t\t\t// Try to abort the upload on state save failure\n1996\t\t\th.backend.AbortMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID)\n1997\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n1998\t\t\treturn\n1999\t\t}\n2000\t\n2001\t\t// Build XML response\n2002\t\ttype InitiateMultipartUploadResult struct {\n2003\t\t\tXMLName  xml.Name `xml:\"InitiateMultipartUploadResult\"`\n2004\t\t\tXmlns    string   `xml:\"xmlns,attr\"`\n2005\t\t\tBucket   string   `xml:\"Bucket\"`\n2006\t\t\tKey      string   `xml:\"Key\"`\n2007\t\t\tUploadID string   `xml:\"UploadId\"`\n2008\t\t}\n2009\t\n2010\t\tresult := InitiateMultipartUploadResult{\n2011\t\t\tXmlns:    \"http://s3.amazonaws.com/doc/2006-03-01/\",\n2012\t\t\tBucket:   bucket,\n2013\t\t\tKey:      key,\n2014\t\t\tUploadID: uploadID,\n2015\t\t}\n2016\t\n2017\t\toutput, err := xml.Marshal(result)\n2018\t\tif err != nil {\n2019\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", \"Failed to marshal response\", 500)\n2020\t\t\treturn\n2021\t\t}\n2022\t\n2023\t\tw.Header().Set(\"Content-Type\", \"application/xml\")\n2024\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2025\t\tw.Write([]byte(`<?xml version=\"1.0\" encoding=\"UTF-8\"?>`))\n2026\t\tw.Write(output)\n2027\t}\n2028\t\n2029\t// UploadPart handles S3 UploadPart with encryption.\n2030\t// Each part is encrypted with a CTR counter offset based on cumulative encrypted bytes.\n2031\tfunc (h *Handlers) UploadPart(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2032\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2033\t\n2034\t\t// Parse part number\n2035\t\tpartNumberStr := r.URL.Query().Get(\"partNumber\")\n2036\t\tif partNumberStr == \"\" {\n2037\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Missing partNumber\", 400)\n2038\t\t\treturn\n2039\t\t}\n2040\t\tpartNumber, err := strconv.ParseInt(partNumberStr, 10, 32)\n2041\t\tif err != nil || partNumber < 1 || partNumber > 10000 {\n2042\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"Invalid partNumber\", 400)\n2043\t\t\treturn\n2044\t\t}\n2045\t\n2046\t\t// Load multipart state\n2047\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2048\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2049\t\tif err != nil {\n2050\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2051\t\t\treturn\n2052\t\t}\n2053\t\n2054\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2055\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2056\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2057\t\t\treturn\n2058\t\t}\n2059\t\n2060\t\t// Read plaintext part\n2061\t\tplaintext, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n2062\t\tif err != nil {\n2063\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n2064\t\t\treturn\n2065\t\t}\n2066\t\n2067\t\tplaintextSize := int64(len(plaintext))\n2068\t\n2069\t\t// VALIDATION: Reject unsupported multipart patterns that would cause silent corruption\n2070\t\t// See docs/upload-retrieval-test-matrix.md U6/U7/U8 and bead bf-59unr3\n2071\t\n2072\t\t// U7: Reject part retries (same partNumber uploaded more than once)\n2073\t\tif _, exists := state.PartSizes[int(partNumber)]; exists {\n2074\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidPart\",\n2075\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Part %d has already been uploaded. ARMOR does not support part retries due to CTR counter derivation from cumulative bytes. Retry the entire multipart upload from the beginning.\", partNumber), 400)\n2076\t\t\treturn\n2077\t\t}\n2078\t\n2079\t\t// U6: Reject out-of-order parts (parts must be uploaded sequentially)\n2080\t\t// The expected part number is derived from the count of already-uploaded parts\n2081\t\texpectedPartNumber := len(state.PartSizes) + 1\n2082\t\tif int(partNumber) != expectedPartNumber {\n2083\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidPartOrder\",\n2084\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Parts must be uploaded in sequential order. Expected part %d, got part %d. ARMOR does not support out-of-order or concurrent part uploads due to CTR counter derivation from cumulative bytes.\", expectedPartNumber, partNumber), 400)\n2085\t\t\treturn\n2086\t\t}\n2087\t\n2088\t\t// U8: Reject non-block-aligned intermediate parts\n2089\t\t// All parts except the last must be multiples of BlockSize (65536 bytes for AES)\n2090\t\t// We don't know if this is the last part at upload time, so we enforce alignment for all parts.\n2091\t\t// Clients can work around this by using a part size that's a multiple of BlockSize.\n2092\t\tif plaintextSize > 0 && plaintextSize%int64(state.BlockSize) != 0 {\n2093\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidPartSize\",\n2094\t\t\t\tfmt.Sprintf(\"Part size %d is not a multiple of the block size (%d bytes). ARMOR's CTR counter derivation requires all parts to be block-aligned. Use a part size that's a multiple of %d (e.g., 5,242,880 for 5MiB, 16,777,216 for 16MiB).\", plaintextSize, state.BlockSize, state.BlockSize), 400)\n2095\t\t\treturn\n2096\t\t}\n2097\t\n2098\t\t// Get the MEK for this upload using the key ID from state\n2099\t\tmek, err := h.keyManager.GetMEKByID(state.KeyID)\n2100\t\tif err != nil {\n2101\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to get decryption key: %v\", err), 500)\n2102\t\t\treturn\n2103\t\t}\n2104\t\n2105\t\t// Unwrap DEK\n2106\t\tdek, err := crypto.UnwrapDEK(mek, state.WrappedDEK)\n2107\t\tif err != nil {\n2108\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to unwrap DEK: %v\", err), 500)\n2109\t\t\treturn\n2110\t\t}\n2111\t\n2112\t\t// Calculate starting block index for CTR counter\n2113\t\t// Each block is blockSize bytes, so counter = encryptedBytes / blockSize\n2114\t\tstartBlockIndex := uint32(state.EncryptedBytes / int64(state.BlockSize))\n2115\t\n2116\t\t// Create encryptor\n2117\t\tencryptor, err := crypto.NewEncryptor(dek, state.IV, state.BlockSize)\n2118\t\tif err != nil {\n2119\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to create encryptor: %v\", err), 500)\n2120\t\t\treturn\n2121\t\t}\n2122\t\n2123\t\t// Encrypt the part with the correct starting counter\n2124\t\t// For multipart uploads, each part continues the CTR stream from where\n2125\t\t// the previous part left off to maintain cryptographic continuity\n2126\t\tencrypted, blockHMACsRaw, err := encryptor.EncryptWithStartingCounter(plaintext, startBlockIndex)\n2127\t\tif err != nil {\n2128\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to encrypt: %v\", err), 500)\n2129\t\t\treturn\n2130\t\t}\n2131\t\n2132\t\t// Upload encrypted part to B2\n2133\t\tetag, err := h.backend.UploadPart(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, int32(partNumber), bytes.NewReader(encrypted), int64(len(encrypted)))\n2134\t\tif err != nil {\n2135\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to upload part: %v\", err), 500)\n2136\t\t\treturn\n2137\t\t}\n2138\t\n2139\t\t// Split concatenated HMACs into individual HMACs for storage\n2140\t\t// The EncryptWithStartingCounter method returns HMACs as a flat byte slice\n2141\t\tblockCount := len(blockHMACsRaw) / crypto.HMACSize\n2142\t\tblockHMACs := make([][]byte, blockCount)\n2143\t\tfor i := 0; i < blockCount; i++ {\n2144\t\t\tblockHMACs[i] = blockHMACsRaw[i*crypto.HMACSize : (i+1)*crypto.HMACSize]\n2145\t\t}\n2146\t\n2147\t\t// Update state with cumulative bytes and part HMACs\n2148\t\tstate.EncryptedBytes += int64(len(encrypted))\n2149\t\tstate.PartHMACs[int(partNumber)] = backend.EncodeHMACToBase64(blockHMACs)\n2150\t\tstate.PartSizes[int(partNumber)] = plaintextSize\n2151\t\n2152\t\tif err := manager.SaveState(ctx, state); err != nil {\n2153\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update multipart state: %v\", err), 500)\n2154\t\t\treturn\n2155\t\t}\n2156\t\n2157\t\tw.Header().Set(\"ETag\", etag)\n2158\t\tw.WriteHeader(http.StatusOK)\n2159\t}\n2160\t\n2161\t// CompleteMultipartUpload handles S3 CompleteMultipartUpload.\n2162\t// It assembles the parts in B2 and stores the HMAC table as a sidecar.\n2163\tfunc (h *Handlers) CompleteMultipartUpload(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request, bucket, key, uploadID string) {\n2164\t\tctx := r.Context()\n2165\t\n2166\t\t// Load multipart state\n2167\t\tmanager := backend.NewMultipartStateManager(h.backend, bucket)\n2168\t\tstate, err := manager.LoadState(ctx, uploadID)\n2169\t\tif err != nil {\n2170\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Multipart upload not found: %v\", err), 404)\n2171\t\t\treturn\n2172\t\t}\n2173\t\n2174\t\t// Verify bucket and key match\n2175\t\tif state.Bucket != bucket || state.Key != key {\n2176\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"NoSuchUpload\", \"Multipart upload does not match bucket/key\", 404)\n2177\t\t\treturn\n2178\t\t}\n2179\t\n2180\t\t// Parse the CompleteMultipartUpload request XML\n2181\t\ttype Part struct {\n2182\t\t\tPartNumber int    `xml:\"PartNumber\"`\n2183\t\t\tETag       string `xml:\"ETag\"`\n2184\t\t}\n2185\t\n2186\t\ttype CompleteMultipartUploadReq struct {\n2187\t\t\tXMLName xml.Name `xml:\"CompleteMultipartUpload\"`\n2188\t\t\tParts   []Part   `xml:\"Part\"`\n2189\t\t}\n2190\t\n2191\t\tvar completeReq CompleteMultipartUploadReq\n2192\t\tbody, err := io.ReadAll(r.Body)\n2193\t\tif err != nil {\n2194\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to read body: %v\", err), 500)\n2195\t\t\treturn\n2196\t\t}\n2197\t\n2198\t\tif err := xml.Unmarshal(body, &completeReq); err != nil {\n2199\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"MalformedXML\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to parse XML: %v\", err), 400)\n2200\t\t\treturn\n2201\t\t}\n2202\t\n2203\t\tif len(completeReq.Parts) == 0 {\n2204\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InvalidRequest\", \"No parts specified\", 400)\n2205\t\t\treturn\n2206\t\t}\n2207\t\n2208\t\t// Convert to backend.CompletedPart\n2209\t\tparts := make([]backend.CompletedPart, len(completeReq.Parts))\n2210\t\tfor i, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2211\t\t\tparts[i] = backend.CompletedPart{\n2212\t\t\t\tPartNumber: int32(p.PartNumber),\n2213\t\t\t\tETag:       p.ETag,\n2214\t\t\t}\n2215\t\t}\n2216\t\n2217\t\t// Calculate total plaintext size\n2218\t\tvar totalPlaintextSize int64\n2219\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2220\t\t\tif size, ok := state.PartSizes[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2221\t\t\t\ttotalPlaintextSize += size\n2222\t\t\t}\n2223\t\t}\n2224\t\n2225\t\t// Assemble all block HMACs in order\n2226\t\tvar allBlockHMACs [][]byte\n2227\t\tfor _, p := range completeReq.Parts {\n2228\t\t\tif hmacsBase64, ok := state.PartHMACs[p.PartNumber]; ok {\n2229\t\t\t\thmacs, err := backend.DecodeHMACFromBase64(hmacsBase64)\n2230\t\t\t\tif err != nil {\n2231\t\t\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to decode HMACs for part %d: %v\", p.PartNumber, err), 500)\n2232\t\t\t\t\treturn\n2233\t\t\t\t}\n2234\t\t\t\tallBlockHMACs = append(allBlockHMACs, hmacs...)\n2235\t\t\t}\n2236\t\t}\n2237\t\n2238\t\t// Complete the multipart upload in B2\n2239\t\tetag, err := h.backend.CompleteMultipartUpload(ctx, bucket, key, uploadID, parts)\n2240\t\tif err != nil {\n2241\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to complete multipart upload: %v\", err), 500)\n2242\t\t\treturn\n2243\t\t}\n2244\t\n2245\t\t// Store HMAC table as sidecar\n2246\t\tif err := manager.SaveHMACTable(ctx, key, allBlockHMACs, state.BlockSize); err != nil {\n2247\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to save HMAC table: %v\", err), 500)\n2248\t\t\treturn\n2249\t\t}\n2250\t\n2251\t\t// Compute plaintext SHA-256 (we don't have the full plaintext, so use a placeholder)\n2252\t\t// In a full implementation, we'd track SHA during upload\n2253\t\tplaintextSHA := sha256.Sum256([]byte{})\n2254\t\n2255\t\t// Build ARMOR metadata and update via CopyObject\n2256\t\tmeta := (&backend.ARMORMetadata{\n2257\t\t\tVersion:       1,\n2258\t\t\tBlockSize:     state.BlockSize,\n2259\t\t\tPlaintextSize: totalPlaintextSize,\n2260\t\t\tContentType:   state.ContentType,\n2261\t\t\tIV:            state.IV,\n2262\t\t\tWrappedDEK:    state.WrappedDEK,\n2263\t\t\tPlaintextSHA:  hex.EncodeToString(plaintextSHA[:]),\n2264\t\t\tETag:          etag,\n2265\t\t\tKeyID:         state.KeyID,\n2266\t\t}).ToMetadata()\n2267\t\n2268\t\t// Add multipart flag to indicate HMAC table is external\n2269\t\tmeta[\"x-amz-meta-armor-multipart\"] = \"true\"\n2270\t\n2271\t\t// Update metadata via CopyObject\n2272\t\tif err := h.backend.Copy(ctx, bucket, key, bucket, key, meta, true); err != nil {\n2273\t\t\th.writeError(w, \"InternalError\", fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to update metadata: %v\", err), 500)\n2274\t\t\treturn\n2275\t\t}\n2276\t\n2277\t\t// Clean up multipart state\n2278\t\tmanager.DeleteState(ctx, uploadID)\n2279\t\n2280\t\t// Record in manifest for fast metadata lookup (async B2 persistence)\n2281\t\tif h.manifest != nil configured or the key doesn't start with the prefix, returns the key unchanged.\n2799\tfunc (h *Handlers) stripPrefix(key string) string {\n2800\t\tif h.config.Prefix == \"\" {\n2801\t\t\treturn ProvenanceRecorder records uploads in the provenance chain.\ntype ProvenanceRecorder interface {\n\tRecordUpload(ctx context.Context, objectKey, plaintextSHA256, operation string) error\n\tShouldRecord(key string) bool\n}\n\n// ManifestEntry holds the decryption metadata for a tracked object as exposed\n// to handlers. It mirrors manifest.Entry but is defined here to avoid an\n// import cycle between the handlers and manifest packages.\ntype ManifestEntry struct {\n\tPlaintextSize int64\n\tContentType   string\n\tETag          string\n\tLastModified  time.Time\n\tIV            []byte\n\tWrappedDEK    []byte\n\tBlockSize     int\n}\n\n// ManifestRecorder records successful S3 write operations in the manifest\n// index for fast in-memory lookup and enqueues them for async B2 persistence\n// as delta files. Implementations must be safe for concurrent use.\ntype ManifestRecorder interface {\n\t// RecordPut records a successful PutObject or CompleteMultipartUpload.\n\tRecordPut(bucket, key string, size int64, sha256Hex string, iv, wrappedDEK []byte, blockSize int, contentType, etag string)\n\t// RecordDelete records a successful DeleteObject.\n\tRecordDelete(bucket, key string)\n\t// Lookup returns manifest metadata for bucket/key, or (nil, false) if not\n\t// tracked. Used to serve HeadObject and ListObjectVersions from memory\n\t// without a B2 round-trip.\n\tLookup(bucket, key string) (*ManifestEntry, bool)\n}\n\n// Handlers contains all S3 operation handlers.\ntype Handlers struct {\n\tconfig      *config.Config\n\tbackend     backend.Backend\n\tcache       *backend.MetadataCache\n\tfooterCache *backend.FooterCache\n\tlistCache   *backend.ListCache\n\tkeyManager  *keymanager.KeyManager\n\tprovenance  ProvenanceRecorder\n\tmanifest    ManifestRecorder\n}\n\n// New creates a new Handlers instance.\nfunc New(cfg *config.Config, be backend.Backend, cache *backend.MetadataCache, footerCache *backend.FooterCache, km *keymanager.KeyManager, listCache *backend.ListCache) *Handlers {\n\treturn &Handlers{\n\t\tconfig:
